methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 6 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Q-CTRL is a Series B quantum computing software company currently valued at $500M post-money, having raised $132M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies across public and private markets: 3 public firms (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

Valuation analysis relied on a single applicable methodology due to peer-set constraints. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, derives an implied value of $950M from the $500M last post-money round. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while 6 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 companies met narrow same-stage or adjacent-stage filters, making sector-wide median estimates unreliable and potentially misleading.

The single-method triangulation supports a median valuation of $950M, with a supporting range of $808M (low) to $1.1B (high) derived from variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the stability of quantum computing sector multiples given rapid technological development and the limited applicability of later-stage public comparables to Series B valuation. The absence of sufficient same-stage peers constrains confidence in the derived range and warrants sensitivity analysis around the 1.9x multiplier assumption.
